assertionResults.title: Hero explains BETTER in plain language
assertionResults.reason: The first viewport explicitly defines BETTER as a prediction-market intelligence platform with AI signal discovery and one-click Polymarket copy trading.

observations: Hero copy reads: 'BETTER is a prediction-market intelligence platform that combines AI-powered signal discovery with one-click copy trading on Polymarket.'
The explanation includes audience/context ('from curious newcomers to committed whales') rather than relying on slogan-only branding.

assertionResults.title: Hero separates live product reality from future vision
assertionResults.reason: Above the fold, the hero distinguishes live closed-beta Terminal/copy trading from planned vaults, agents, and HyperEVM work, with a nearby directional caveat.

observations: Hero badges show 'Live — Terminal & copy-trading in closed beta' and 'Planned — Vaults, agents & HyperEVM ahead'.
A caveat box directly under the badges says these are directional ambitions, not commitments.

assertionResults.title: Current live scope and freshness are visible
assertionResults.reason: The early live section specifies current capabilities and includes both a status date and BETTER Docs source cue, avoiding generic availability language.

observations: The 'What's Live Today' section states 'Status as of 2026-Q1 · Source: BETTER Docs · docs.betteragent.ai'.
Live items are specific: BETTER Terminal (Closed Beta), Lite Mode, Token Access Gate & FDV Ratchet, Trading Tax Revenue, and Community Signal Sharing.

assertionResults.title: Primary navigation exposes required destinations
assertionResults.reason: Desktop and mobile navigation both expose clear destination links, and tested anchors landed on the intended sections without leaving the page in a broken overlay state.

observations: Desktop nav exposes What is BETTER, Live Now, Roadmap, Tokenomics, Architecture, Evidence & Sources, and Risks & Caveats; tested anchors reached #what-is-better, #roadmap, #evidence, and #risks, and the desktop recording traversed Live Now → Roadmap → Tokenomics → Architecture → Evidence & Sources → Risks & Caveats.
Mobile menu exposes the same labels; tested taps reached #what-is-better, #live-now, #roadmap, #evidence, and #risks.

assertionResults.title: Navigation labels remain understandable without insider context
assertionResults.reason: Primary nav and nearby section labels use common-language destination names rather than BETTER-specific jargon.

observations: Labels such as 'What is BETTER', 'Live Now', 'Roadmap', 'Tokenomics', 'Architecture', 'Evidence & Sources', and 'Risks & Caveats' are understandable to a first-time visitor.
Destination headings reinforce the same plain-language framing: 'What's Live Today', 'Evidence & Sources', and 'Risks & Caveats'.

assertionResults.title: Aggressive claims expose evidence hooks
assertionResults.reason: Claims do show visible BETTER Docs citation hooks, but the tested external BETTER Docs target does not resolve, so the citation path does not successfully reach a claim-relevant source.

observations: Hero and live-state claims display visible 'BETTER Docs as of 2026-Q1' hooks adjacent to the content.

Clicking the BETTER Docs hook opened an error tab instead of source material; browser navigation failed with ERR_NAME_NOT_RESOLVED and curl verification returned 'Could not resolve host: docs.betteragent.ai'.
The local 'Evidence & Sources' section itself is reachable, but the claim-specific external citation target did not resolve during testing.
evidence.consoleErrors: none on the local page; external BETTER Docs link failed to resolve

assertionResults.title: Aggressive and future-facing claims carry nearby caveats or confidence framing
assertionResults.reason: Future-facing claims are paired with immediate caveat and dependency framing in both the hero and roadmap surfaces instead of reading as guaranteed capability.

observations: The hero caveat states: 'These are directional ambitions, not commitments. Timelines, scope, and feasibility depend on market conditions, technical execution, and adoption.'
Roadmap cards include inline caveat banners and dependency notes, and the dedicated Risks & Caveats section reinforces the uncertainty framing.

assertionResults.title: Calls to action are honest about destination, availability, and hierarchy
assertionResults.reason: The primary hero CTA leads to the live/proof section, while the secondary CTA leads to roadmap exploration; the visual hierarchy makes the live path primary.

observations: 'See What's Live' is the prominent filled CTA and lands on the live section rather than implying an unavailable product action.
'Explore the Roadmap' is visually secondary and lands on the roadmap section, matching an exploration promise.

assertionResults.title: First viewport is one poster-like hero composition
assertionResults.reason: The first viewport reads as a single centered hero composition with one dominant brand statement, supporting copy, status chips, caveat, and CTA pair rather than a dashboard cluster.

observations: Above the fold there are no competing stat strips or card grids; the supporting elements all reinforce the same hero statement.
The atmospheric background remains behind a single central narrative stack.

assertionResults.title: BETTER brand is dominant above the fold
assertionResults.reason: The oversized BETTER wordmark and logo make BETTER the unmistakable primary brand signal before any scroll.

observations: The word 'BETTER' is the largest element in the first viewport and sits above the plain-language explanatory line.
Secondary UI elements stay visually subordinate to the BETTER mark and headline.

frictions.description: The repeated external BETTER Docs citation target (`docs.betteragent.ai`) did not resolve from either the browser or curl during validation.
frictions.resolution: Retried via browser navigation and curl; both returned host-resolution failure.

summary: Tested 10 assigned narrative/navigation assertions on the local browser surface. 9 passed and 1 failed: VAL-NARR-008 failed because the visible BETTER Docs evidence hooks did not resolve to a reachable source target. No local-page console/runtime errors were observed beyond normal React DevTools/HMR info logs.
